### Question B — [KTU University Exam — Dec 2023, Module 4]

**(a)** Describe the **risk assessment methodology** prescribed by **ISO 12100:2010** for machinery safety. Use the hierarchy of controls in your answer. **(7 Marks)**

**Model Solution:**

**Introduction:**
ISO 12100:2010 is the **horizontal-type safety standard** for machinery that provides a **general design principle** for risk assessment and risk reduction. It applies to all machinery regardless of industry.

**The 3-Step Risk Assessment Process:**

**Step 1 — Determine the Limits of the Machinery:**
- **Use limits:** Intended use + reasonably foreseeable misuse.
- **Space limits:** Range of movement, installation space, human reach.
- **Time limits:** Service life, maintenance intervals.
- **Environmental limits:** Temperature, humidity, dust, vibration.

**Step 2 — Hazard Identification:**
The standard identifies **seven hazard categories:**

1. Mechanical (crushing, shearing, cutting, entanglement)
2. Electrical (shock, arc flash, static)
3. Thermal (burns, hot surfaces)
4. Noise (hearing damage)
5. Vibration (HAVS — Hand-Arm Vibration Syndrome)
6. Radiation (ionizing, non-ionizing)
7. Material/Substance (toxic dust, fumes)

**Step 3 — Risk Estimation & Evaluation:**

$$R = f(S, L, P)$$

Where:
- $S$ = Severity of harm
- $L$ = Likelihood of occurrence
- $P$ = Possibility of avoidance

**The Hierarchy of Controls (in order of effectiveness):**

1. **Eliminate** — Remove the hazard entirely (e.g., remove a rotating shaft instead of guarding it).
2. **Substitute** — Replace with lesser hazard (e.g., hydraulic fluid with less toxic fluid).
3. **Engineering Controls** — Guards, interlocks, light curtains, two-hand controls.
4. **Administrative Controls** — Procedures, training, signage, work permits.
5. **PPE** — Gloves, helmets, hearing protection (last resort).

**Re-evaluation:** After each control, risk must be **re-estimated** until it is reduced to an acceptable level under the **ALARP** principle.

**Conclusion:**
ISO 12100 provides a **systematic, iterative, and documented** methodology that has become the global gold standard for machinery safety, forming the basis for **CE marking** in the EU and BIS certification in India.

**(b)** A worker is exposed to noise of **95 dB(A)** for **4 hours** and **80 dB(A)** for **4 hours** during an 8-hour shift. Calculate the **Normalized Continuous Noise Exposure Level (Lex,8h)** using ISO 1999 and state whether PPE is required. **(7 Marks)**

**Model Solution:**

**Step 1 — Recall the ISO 1999 formula:**

$$L_{ex,8h} = 10 \log_{10} \left[ \frac{1}{T_{0}} \sum_{i=1}^{n} t_{i} \cdot 10^{L_{i}/10} \right]$$

Where:
- $T_0 = 8$ hours
- $t_i$ = duration of exposure at level $L_i$
- $L_i$ = noise level in dB(A)

**Step 2 — Substitute values:**

$$L_{ex,8h} = 10 \log_{10} \left[ \frac{1}{8} \left( 4 \cdot 10^{95/10} + 4 \cdot 10^{80/10} \right) \right]$$

**Step 3 — Compute the exponential terms:**

$$10^{95/10} = 10^{9.5} = 3.162 \times 10^{9}$$

$$10^{80/10} = 10^{8.0} = 1.000 \times 10^{8}$$

**Step 4 — Multiply by time and sum:**

$$4 \times 3.162 \times 10^{9} = 1.265 \times 10^{10}$$

$$4 \times 1.000 \times 10^{8} = 4.000 \times 10^{8}$$

$$\text{Sum} = 1.265 \times 10^{10} + 0.040 \times 10^{10} = 1.305 \times 10^{10}$$

**Step 5 — Divide by reference time:**

$$\frac{1.305 \times 10^{10}}{8} = 1.631 \times 10^{9}$$

**Step 6 — Take the logarithm:**

$$L_{ex,8h} = 10 \log_{10} (1.631 \times 10^{9})$$

$$L_{ex,8h} = 10 \left[ \log_{10}(1.631) + 9 \right]$$

$$L_{ex,8h} = 10 \left[ 0.2125 + 9 \right]$$

$$L_{ex,8h} = 10 \times 9.2125 = 92.13 \, \text{dB(A)}$$

**Result:** $L_{ex,8h} \approx 92.1 \, \text{dB(A)}$

**Step 7 — Compare with ISO 45001 / OSHA Threshold:**
- Permissible exposure limit = **85 dB(A)** for 8 hours.
- Action level = **80 dB(A)**.
- $L_{ex,8h} = 92.1 \, \text{dB(A)}$ exceeds **both** thresholds.

**Step 8 — Required Action:**
1. **Engineering controls** first — install noise enclosures, vibration dampers, or replace noisy machinery.
2. **Administrative controls** — rotate workers to limit exposure duration.
3. **Mandatory PPE** — earplugs + earmuffs (dual protection) with documented fit-test records.
4. **Audiometric testing** — periodic hearing tests for affected workers under the Occupational Health program.

**Conclusion:**
The exposure is **7.1 dB(A) above** the permissible limit. Under ISO 45001 Clause 8.1.2 and the Hierarchy of Controls, the employer must implement engineering and administrative controls **before** relying on PPE.

> [!WARNING]
> **KTU Examiner's Valuation Warning — Common Pitfalls:**
> 1. ❌ Writing only "ISO 45001 is for safety" without naming it as an OH&S management system and replacing OHSAS 18001 — **2 marks lost**.
> 2. ❌ Confusing **OHSAS 18001** with **ISO 45001** in the comparison question — examiners deduct heavily.
> 3. ❌ In LTIFR calculation, forgetting to multiply by **10^6** — silent mark-killer.
> 4. ❌ In noise calculation, **not converting dB(A) into antilog** form $10^{L/10}$ before averaging — almost all students lose 3 marks here.
> 5. ❌ Listing PPE as the **first** control — wrong order; must follow Hierarchy of Controls.
> 6. ❌ Writing "ISO 14001 is a safety standard" — it is an **environmental** management standard.

## 📌 Topic Recap & Important Things to Remember (Rapid Revision Checklist)

- ✅ **ISO** = International Organization for Standardization, headquartered in **Geneva, Switzerland**, founded **1946**.
- ✅ **ISO 45001:2018** = OH&S management system; replaced **OHSAS 18001:2007** in March 2021.
- ✅ **Annex SL** = 10-clause High-Level Structure shared by ISO 9001, 14001, 45001 — enables **Integrated Management System (IMS)**.
- ✅ **PDCA Cycle:** Plan (Clauses 4–6) → Do (Clauses 7–8) → Check (Clause 9) → Act (Clause 10).
- ✅ **ISO 12100:2010** = Safety of Machinery; risk = f(Severity × Likelihood).
- ✅ **ISO 13849-1** uses **Performance Levels (PL a → PL e)** for safety-related control systems.
- ✅ **ISO 31000** = generic risk management guideline; **not certifiable**.
- ✅ **Hierarchy of Controls (Clause 8.1.2):** Eliminate → Substitute → Engineering → Administrative → PPE.
- ✅ **LTIFR** = (Lost-Time Injuries × 10⁶) / Man-Hours Worked.
- ✅ **TRIR** = (Recordable Injuries × 200,000) / Man-Hours Worked.
- ✅ **Risk Score** $R = S \times L$; classify as Low (≤4), Medium (5–12), High (≥15).
- ✅ **Noise threshold** = 85 dB(A) Lex,8h; above this → engineering controls + PPE mandatory.
- ✅ **Worker Participation (Clause 5.4)** is **mandatory** under ISO 45001 — a key departure from OHSAS 18001.
- ✅ **Context of the Organization (Clause 4)** and **Risks & Opportunities (Clause 6.1)** are the **newest concepts** introduced.
- ✅ Always state the **year of revision** when naming ISO standards (e.g., ISO 45001:**2018**).
- ✅ Integrated audits (QMS + EMS + OH&S) reduce total audit cost by **30–40%** for organizations.
